fresh_facts.claim: US and Israeli air campaigns have targeted Iranian military infrastructure including ballistic missile sites, IRGC bases, air defenses, and nuclear-related facilities, with hundreds of strikes recorded across 26 provinces since early March 2026.
The Pentagon declared Iran's navy 'gone' on March 19, 2026, with over 15,000 total targets struck during the campaign.
US military deployments in the Middle East escalated to 50,000 troops as of late March 2026 to support ongoing operations.
Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered on March 25, 2026 to maximize destruction of Iran's arms industry within 48 hours.
Israeli strikes hit four key ballistic missile production facilities by March 29, 2026, including the Imam Javad Missile Base near Shiraz.
Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed early in the military campaign according to intelligence reports.
Iran's IRGC issued orders to evacuate border cities like Mariwan on March 3, 2026, signaling preparations for ground confrontation.
Iran retaliated with missile and drone barrages targeting all GCC states (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) and US bases.
A US 15-point proposal was delivered to Iran on March 24, 2026 via Pakistan demanding nuclear dismantlement and missile limits.
The White House issued threats of further action against Iran if the 15-point proposal demands were not met as of March 25, 2026.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed Supreme National Security Council Secretary after Ali Larijani's killing on March 17, 2026, signaling regime shift to full IRGC control.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts amid the ongoing military campaign.
IRGC Tehran commander Hassan Hassanzadeh was killed, undermining capital security control.
Strikes halted Foolad Khuzestan steel production and damaged electrical grids, causing blackouts in Tehran as of late March 2026.
A nationwide internet blackout in Iran reached day 31 by late March 2026.
Trump warned on March 30, 2026 that the US would seize Iran's Kharg Island oil hub if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
UK Prime Minister Starmer stated on March 30, 2026 that Britain will not join the war against Iran.

US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman General Dan Caine held an urgent Pentagon briefing on March 31, 2026 regarding Iran operations, diplomatic negotiations, and regional stability.
The Iranian regime intensified controls with arbitrary arrests of teachers' union activists, VPN users, and civilians via home raids and device confiscations.
A London rally on March 28, 2026 urged UK recognition of the NCRI provisional government and Maryam Rajavi's 10-point democratic plan.
Trump stated that combined US-Israeli forces struck 13,000 targets since the start of the war as reported by Financial Times on March 30, 2026.
Extensive strikes in the past 24 hours of March 2026 targeted Iranian nuclear and military sites including Khondab, Parchin, Kohak, and ballistic missile facilities.
The US deployed additional special forces including Rangers and SEALs to the Middle East, raising regional troop levels above 50,000 by late March 2026.
Iran conducted strikes on the Israeli Haifa refinery, causing a fire on March 30, 2026.
Iran's attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure in Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar risk systemic global economic realignment as of late March 2026.
The Strait of Hormuz disruption severely impacts global oil flows as of late March 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: US-Israeli military campaign has executed 13,000+ strikes across Iranian military, nuclear, and economic infrastructure while Iran retaliates against regional targets; the regime faces simultaneous internal collapse with Khamenei's death, IRGC consolidation under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, widespread infrastructure degradation, internet blackout, and economic paralysis including Strait of Hormuz closure.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pentagon briefing on March 31, 2026 addressed ongoing Iran operations and diplomatic negotiations as US military pressure reaches critical levels with 50,000+ troops deployed and threats to seize Kharg Island if Strait of Hormuz remains closed.
